change_summary: detail_evidence expanded from 2297 to 7617 chars (+232%), detail_verdict from 264 to 1583 chars (+499%). Major content additions: dome's 'complementary' defense quoted and rebutted, H(r) supremum argument, formula-switching analysis, three-way inconsistency, Chapel Hill height discrepancy, Schumann-Tesla correlation circularity. All C2 major/moderate recommendations were implemented.
our_argument_summary: Our review now presents a comprehensive, multi-layered case: (1) The dome's own H(r) gives 22-35 Hz, not 7.83 Hz. (2) The 10.59 Hz is Schumann's 1952 globe formula. (3) The monitoring code hardcodes pred=obs=7.83. (4) The dome's explicit 'complementary' defense (WIN-002 + WIN-029) fails because lossy cavity inversion recovers effective acoustic height, not geometric height. (5) The H(r) supremum of 8,537 km makes both 9,086 and 9,572 km mathematically unreachable by any average. (6) The 'reconciliation' requires switching between incompatible formulas (globe circumferential vs dome quarter-wave). (7) The Schumann-Tesla r=0.999 correlation is trivially produced by two hardcoded constants.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 1 in the live power dashboard states: Predicted=7.83 Hz, Observed=7.83 Hz, Tolerance=5.0%, Status=PASS. No derivation chain connects dome geometry to the predicted value. The 'derivation' listed is f = c/(4H₀) → H₀ = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km — an inversion from the measured value to a height, not a prediction from dome geometry to a frequency.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Entirely hardcoded. Both predicted and observed are static 7.83 Hz. The cross-domain correlation matrix lists Schumann ↔ Tesla at r=0.999, but both values are static constants, making the correlation tautological.
data_sources_used: None for Domain 1. The falsification threshold ('Frequency drifts >0.3 Hz for >1 hour') implies awareness that Schumann varies, but no live data feed tracks this.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Fixed 5.0% tolerance with 0.0% error. The falsification threshold ('drifts >0.3 Hz for >1 hour') is never tested because no live Schumann data is fetched.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our characterization of the code as tautological is verified. The dashboard text confirms Domain 1 is a static PASS.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Domain 1 in live-power dashboard: Predicted=7.83 Hz, Observed=7.83 Hz, Status=PASS. No external API call, no formula evaluation. The 'derivation' shown is an inversion (measured frequency → height), not a prediction (dome geometry → frequency).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: 10.59 Hz is Schumann's 1952 globe formula f₁ = (c/2πa)√2 with a = 6,371 km. The 26% gap is standard ionospheric conductivity loss (Sentman 1995). Dome renames this 'aetheric damping' without changing numerical content.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Schumann predicted 10.59 Hz in 1952. Balser & Wagner measured 7.83 Hz in 1960. Both values were established 60+ years before the dome model. The 26% gap was computed from pre-existing values.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 10.59 Hz requires Earth's radius (6,371 km) — a globe parameter. The dome's own quarter-wave formula f=c/(4H) with H₀=8,537 km gives 8.78 Hz, not 10.59 Hz. No dome parameter produces 10.59 Hz. The 26% damping is a free parameter with no aetheric derivation.

holes_found.description: The text states the dome evolution page lists three heights it says H(r) harmonizes: '9,086 km (model parameterization, stated to match back-derived Schumann to 5.3%), 9,572 km (WIN-029's c/(4 × 7.83) inversion), and 4,750 km (Polaris).' However, the dome's source text (05-model.txt) lists these as 'Schumann resonance (~9,500 km) = pole-proximal firmament (r ~ 0)' — i.e., the dome associates the ~9,500 km with pole-proximal measurement, NOT with 9,086 km (which it labels 'near-pole average'). Our text correctly identifies these as separate values but the phrase 'stated to match back-derived Schumann to 5.3%' is our characterization that conflates two different items the dome lists. The 5.3% figure comes from the evolution text (06-evolution.txt line 68) comparing 9,572 km to 9,086 km, which is accurate. But the way our text presents the three heights doesn't perfectly mirror the dome's own three-item list. A pedantic defender could say we're misrepresenting the dome's categorization.
holes_found.recommendation: Minor clarification: When listing the three heights the dome claims to reconcile, match the dome's own categorization: 'Schumann resonance (~9,500 km), Polaris geometric (4,750 km), and model parameterization (9,086 km).' The 5.3% comparison between 9,572 and 9,086 can follow as a separate sentence. This is cosmetic — the mathematical argument (both exceed the 8,537 km supremum) is unaffected.

holes_found.description: The new text includes the claim about 'formula-switching' between globe circumferential modes (f₁ = c/(2πR)√2) and dome quarter-wave (f = c/(4H)). While correct, a technically literate dome defender could argue that both formulas describe standing waves — the first in the circumferential direction, the second in the radial direction — and that a real cavity has modes in both directions, so invoking two formulas isn't inherently contradictory. The weakness is that the dome never establishes a coherent mode structure that uses BOTH formulas simultaneously; it cherry-picks whichever gives a convenient number. Our text should emphasize the cherry-picking rather than the incompatibility of the formulas per se.
holes_found.recommendation: No change strictly needed — the current text already says 'The dome's reconciliation requires these two formulas to describe the same cavity, but they describe geometrically incompatible systems.' The key word 'incompatible' could be softened to 'distinct' to avoid the pedantic objection, but the argument stands: using the circumferential formula (globe) for the 10.59 Hz starting point and the vertical formula (dome) for the 9,572 km inversion yields an incoherent physics.

holes_found.description: The post-hoc paragraph in detail_verdict_text refers to 'Part 3.1b' via an anchor link. The V6 restructure map shows '3.1' maps to a WINs-reviewed subsection, and 'Part 3.1b' would be a sub-subsection. Verify that the anchor #posthoc-standard resolves correctly after the restructure. If Part 3.1b was renamed or moved, the link could be broken.
holes_found.recommendation: Integrity agent should verify #posthoc-standard anchor resolves. Not a content issue.

stronger_arguments.description: The current text has a strong quantitative argument about different damping ratios (26% from globe formula vs 11% from dome's own H₀). This could be made even more pointed: if the dome accepts that its cavity medium has a loss tangent that produces 26% frequency reduction, then the effective height inversion should reflect this loss tangent. Specifically, in a cavity with complex permittivity ε* = ε' - jε'', the resonant frequency shifts as f_real = f_0 × √(1 - 1/(2Q)²) where Q is the quality factor. With Q ≈ 4 (corresponding to 26% loss), the height inversion error would be ~6%, making the '5.3% match' between 9,572 and 9,086 km no better than the expected error from the lossy medium itself. The 'match' is within the noise of the effect the dome invokes to explain it.
sources: Standard cavity resonator theory: Pozar, Microwave Engineering, Chapter 6

kernel_of_truth.description: The 26% gap between idealized Schumann prediction and measurement is real and historically significant. The idea that the propagation medium causes the discrepancy is correct — standard physics agrees (it's the lossy ionosphere). The dome model also correctly notes that Schumann resonance requires a conductive ceiling, which is accurate for both the ionosphere and a hypothetical metallic dome. The attempt to derive firmament height from frequency (c/(4f)) is dimensionally correct as a characteristic length.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The dome borrows the globe's prediction (10.59 Hz from Earth's radius), the globe's measurement (7.83 Hz), and the globe's qualitative explanation (medium loss), then renames them all. The dome's own geometry gives 22-35 Hz. The quarter-wave inversion gives 9,572 km — exceeding the dome's own ceiling. No consistent physics connects the dome's parameters to the observed frequency. The 26% is a free parameter, not a derivation.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender could argue: 'Your own review admits the 26% gap is real and that standard physics attributes it to medium losses — exactly what the dome claims. You say the dome's formula gives 22-35 Hz, but that's using spatial averages over the entire disc. Schumann resonances are excited primarily in the pole-proximal region where H(r) ≈ 8,537 km, which gives 8.78 Hz — much closer to 7.83 Hz than your 22-35 Hz scare numbers. The dome's H(r) is designed to be locally evaluated, not globally averaged. The 11% residual (8.78 → 7.83 Hz) is well within what aetheric medium losses would produce — even your standard physics says the medium causes a ~26% shift. Furthermore, the supremum argument is a red herring: the dome says 9,086 km is a "near-pole average" which could be a misstatement in the prose, not a mathematical claim. The dome's key physical claim — that a conductive cavity ceiling at ~8,500 km produces Schumann-like resonance near 7.83 Hz — is actually closer to correct than the globe's idealized 10.59 Hz starting point.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The defense's strongest point is that pole-proximal H(r) ≈ 8,537 km gives 8.78 Hz, which is closer to 7.83 Hz than our 22-35 Hz figures suggest. We should explicitly acknowledge this in the text and explain why it doesn't help: (a) Schumann modes are NOT localized near the pole — they're global standing waves that average over the entire cavity; the fundamental mode has a wavelength comparable to the cavity circumference (b) even using H₀ = 8,537 locally, the quarter-wave formula gives 8.78 Hz, not 7.83 Hz, so the dome still needs aetheric damping — just 11% instead of 26%, a different number than claimed (c) the dome's own text specifically invokes the 10.59 Hz and 26% — if 8.78 Hz and 11% are more correct, the dome undermines its own WIN by using the wrong starting point. We partially address (c) already but points (a) and (b) should be made more explicit.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No inconsistencies in our own arguments across these WINs. WIN-002 says the dome's Schumann frequency doesn't derive from dome geometry; WIN-029 says the height inversion exceeds the dome's ceiling; WIN-038 says the Schumann persistence is standard physics. These reinforce each other. WIN-001 (Tesla 11.78 Hz) is cited as correlating with Schumann at r=0.999 in the dome's dashboard — our review correctly calls this tautological (two hardcoded constants). The connection is consistent with our WIN-001 analysis.

quantitative_verification.computations: All verified via Python. c = 299,792.458 km/s, a = 6,371 km, H₀ = 8,537 km, L = 8,619 km, R = 20,015 km, j₀₁ = 2.4048. Every numerical claim in our text matches computed values to rounding precision. H(5960) = 4,276 km (our text says 4,274 km — difference is rounding in the exponent, negligible). Area-weighted H_eff = 2,135 km (text says '~2,140 km' — consistent).

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 3 change-driven review. The C2 major finding (dome's 'complementary' defense unaddressed) and C2 moderate finding (H(r) supremum argument) have both been fully incorporated into the text. The detail_evidence has tripled in length, adding: (1) direct quotation and rebuttal of the dome's 'complementary' defense from 06-evolution.txt, (2) the lossy cavity inversion argument (damped frequency recovers effective acoustic height, not geometric height), (3) the three-way inconsistency between 10.59 Hz, 7.83 Hz, and H(0)=8,537 km, (4) the H(r) supremum bound proving 9,086 and 9,572 km are mathematically unreachable, (5) the Chapel Hill height discrepancy (4,750 vs 4,274 km), (6) the formula-switching critique (globe circumferential vs dome quarter-wave), and (7) the Schumann-Tesla correlation circularity.

All mathematics verified computationally. All source claims verified against dome text. The text is now substantially stronger than at C2. The Self-Contradicted verdict is well-supported by multiple independent lines of argument, any one of which suffices.

The one moderate finding concerns the presentation order of the dome's three reconciliation heights — our text conflates the dome's categorization slightly. This is cosmetic and doesn't affect the mathematical argument. The advocate mode finding (defense_survives=3) identifies a genuinely tricky rhetorical point: pole-proximal evaluation gives 8.78 Hz, closer to 7.83 than our 22-35 Hz spatial averages. Our text partially addresses this but could be more explicit about why local evaluation is physically wrong for Schumann modes (they're global standing waves, not local oscillations). Recommending this as a minor edit for a future pass.

The detail_verdict_text now includes a post-hoc analysis paragraph linking to Part 3.1b, which is a good cross-reference addition from the expansion pipeline.

Overall assessment: WIN-002 is now one of the strongest entries in the review. The multiple independent failure modes (wrong geometry, borrowed formula, hardcoded monitoring, self-contradicting height reconciliation, formula-switching) make this nearly impervious to challenge. Confidence raised to 0.97.
